news.headline: NeurAxis Declares Q2 2026 Stock Dividend on Series B Preferred
news.summary: NeurAxis's board authorized a Q2 2026 stock dividend on its Series B Preferred Stock, to be paid in roughly 80,463 common shares rather than cash. The dividend is payable July 29, 2026 to holders of record as of July 21, 2026, based on a $2.38 conversion rate.

news.headline: NeurAxis Stockholders Approve Directors and Equity Compensation Plans
news.summary: At its June 10, 2026 annual meeting, NeurAxis shareholders re-elected all six director nominees and approved expanding the 2022 Omnibus Incentive Plan's share pool from about 1.48 million to 3.4 million shares, plus a new 2025 Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

news.headline: NeurAxis Reports Record First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results Driven by 80% Revenue Growth
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue hit a record $1.6 million, up 80% year-over-year, with gross margin expanding to 86.4% and net loss narrowing to $1.8 million, beating consensus estimates. Management credited the new Category I CPT code for PENFS reimbursement and early VA Federal Supply Schedule adoption running ahead of plan.

news.headline: NeurAxis Expands Payer Coverage with Four New Medical Policies
news.summary: NeurAxis announced four additional insurer medical policies covering PENFS/IB-Stim, adding roughly 1.25 million covered lives across West Virginia, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Virginia and Florida. The move continues the company's push to broaden reimbursement access ahead of the Category I CPT code taking effect.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings call
catalysts.detail: NeurAxis is scheduled to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and host a business update call.

catalysts.label: Series B preferred stock dividend payment
catalysts.detail: The already-declared Q2 2026 stock dividend (~80,463 common shares) pays out to Series B preferred holders of record as of July 21, 2026.

catalysts.label: Additional payer medical-policy wins
catalysts.detail: Company continues pursuing new insurer coverage decisions for PENFS/IB-Stim to further expand covered lives beyond current levels.

catalysts.label: VA Federal Supply Schedule ramp
catalysts.detail: Continued scale-up of IB-Stim sales through the VA's Federal Supply Schedule contract, which management said is tracking ahead of initial expectations.

pulse: NeurAxis continues to post strong top-line momentum from its IB-Stim/PENFS neuromodulation device, with Q1 2026 revenue up 80% year-over-year on expanding insurance reimbursement (new Category I CPT code) and VA Federal Supply Schedule adoption running ahead of plan. The stock trades in the $7 area (52-week range roughly $2.20-$9.33, market cap near $85 million), well below Craig-Hallum's $13 price target, as losses narrow toward management's targeted sub-$1 million quarterly cash burn. Near-term news flow has been incremental (routine Series B dividend, June annual-meeting governance approvals, payer coverage expansions), with the next major catalyst being Q2 2026 earnings on September 17.
